title: Automotive T1 ABM Ramp — Ford & Stellantis
body: 5 T1 auto accounts $116M below plan. Ford (RFP Aug 12, −$30M) and Stellantis (carrier review open, −$24M) are critical. GM playbook activated — 1-to-1 ABM, NAAF pre-lock, account team restored.

decision_tree.label: Automotive T1 Revenue Gap
decision_tree.sub: $116M below plan across 5 T1 auto accounts — Ford and Stellantis most critical

decision_tree.label: External: FedEx Auto Express
decision_tree.sub: FedEx launched USMCA cross-border product Jun 5. Pricing 8% below UPS list. Targeting Ford + Stellantis plant managers.

decision_tree.label: External: Ford 8-K Production Delay
decision_tree.sub: Ford BlueOval City TN production start delayed to Aug 2026. Battery logistics RFP closes Aug 12. ADV gap began Q4 2025.

decision_tree.label: Internal: CRM Account Gap Data
decision_tree.sub: Ford: −$30M vs plan, account team cut 2.5→1.0 FTE Jan 2026. Stellantis: −$24M vs plan, procurement reorg weakened exec relationships.

decision_tree.label: Internal: ABM Memory — GM Analog
decision_tree.sub: INI-2025-027 (GM Detroit): CCO briefings + Industry Expert + NAAF capacity lock lifted engagement +47%. SOW growing. 3 of 4 quarters completed.

decision_tree.label: Companion Correlates
decision_tree.sub: HIGH — $54M combined gap · 2 accounts · 6-day FedEx timing advantage. GM playbook is highest-confidence analog — same OEM T1 profile, same FedEx threat, same geography.

decision_tree.label: Options: NAAF Pre-lock / Price Match / Wait
decision_tree.sub: A: NAAF pre-lock + GM ABM playbook (+$40M P50). B: Price match FedEx −8% (margin erosion ~$8M). C: Monitor and respond to RFP (purely reactive).

decision_tree.label: Matt Selected: Option A — NAAF Pre-lock
decision_tree.sub: VP Automotive Sales instructed to route NAAF pre-lock offer to Ford and Stellantis within 48 hours. GM analog removes uncertainty about playbook effectiveness.

decision_tree.label: In Reality
decision_tree.sub: Ford RFP closes Aug 12. Stellantis procurement VP meeting scheduled. NAAF capacity confirmed 85% ready. Account team restored to 2.0 FTE at Ford.

decision_tree.label: Next Gate
decision_tree.sub: Both accounts to respond by Aug 31. Close rate vs +$40M P50 modeled lift is tracking metric. CCO briefing cadence activated monthly.

activity_log.change: $116M cumulative revenue gap flagged across 5 T1 auto accounts — Ford and Stellantis most critical
activity_log.notes: Gap exceeds quarterly tolerance threshold — triggered executive review

activity_log.change: FedEx launched USMCA cross-border Auto Express product Jun 5 — pricing 8% below UPS list, targeting Ford + Stellantis plant managers
activity_log.notes: Direct threat to exposed UPS lanes in Detroit-3 corridor

activity_log.change: Ford BlueOval City TN production delayed to Aug 2026. Battery logistics RFP closes Aug 12. ADV gap began Q4 2025
activity_log.notes: RFP window creates urgency — FedEx could secure forward commitment before Aug

activity_log.change: Ford: −$30M vs plan, account team cut 2.5→1.0 FTE Jan 2026. Stellantis: −$24M, procurement reorg weakened exec relationships
activity_log.notes: Coverage ratio 3x below healthy threshold — structural gap, not seasonal

activity_log.change: GM Detroit playbook (INI-2025-027): CCO briefings + NAAF capacity lock lifted engagement +47%. SOW growing across 3 of 4 quarters
activity_log.notes: Proven analog exists — reduces execution risk of proposed strategy

activity_log.change: HIGH — $54M combined gap, 2 accounts at risk, 6-day FedEx timing advantage. NAAF pre-lock eliminates FedEx timing advantage by offering August launch as present commercial offer
activity_log.notes: Convergence of internal weakness and external aggression requires immediate response

activity_log.change: 3 paths: A) NAAF pre-lock + GM ABM playbook (+$40M P50), B) Price match FedEx −8% (margin erosion ~$8M), C) Monitor and respond to RFP (reactive)
activity_log.notes: Each option addresses different risk dimension — capability vs price vs wait

activity_log.change: Matt approved Option A. VP Automotive Sales instructed to route NAAF pre-lock offer to Ford and Stellantis account teams within 48 hours
activity_log.notes: GM analog removes uncertainty. NAAF pre-lock is only move defeating FedEx on timing. Price matching gives margin away; waiting gives accounts away

activity_log.change: NAAF pre-lock offer routed. Account team restored to 2.0 FTE at Ford. Stellantis procurement VP meeting scheduled
activity_log.notes: NAAF capacity confirmed 85% ready — operational bottleneck cleared

activity_log.change: Ford RFP closes Aug 12. Stellantis carrier review open. NAAF capacity 85% ready. CCO briefing cadence activated monthly
activity_log.notes: Both accounts to respond by Aug 31 — close rate vs +$40M P50 is tracking metric

title: AutoZone Renewal Save-Play — L2 Override Decision
body: AutoZone ($41M, Pkt 10941) facing FedEx counter-offer. Analyst staged S3 (floor breach, OR 0.70) but S2 (Happy Returns bundle, OR 0.67) is viable within guardrails. CCO sign-off required.

decision_tree.label: AutoZone $41M Renewal at Risk
decision_tree.sub: FedEx counter-offer received. Analyst staged override (S3) but compliant alternative (S2) exists.

decision_tree.label: External: AutoZone Q3 Earnings
decision_tree.sub: AutoZone publicly stated 'reviewing carrier diversification.' FedEx counter-offer at −8.5% base discount with 24-month lock.

decision_tree.label: Internal: FedEx Counter Pricing
decision_tree.sub: FedEx pricing at −8.5% base on aftermarket lanes. Time-bound offer. Aftermarket peak season Aug–Oct narrows retention window.

decision_tree.label: Internal: Deal Analyser — Pkt 10941
decision_tree.sub: Analyst T. Whitaker staged S0–S3. S3 matches FedEx at OR 0.70 — breaches L2 ceiling TARGET 81.1%→143%. S2: Happy Returns bundle, OR 0.67, profit $14.4M.

decision_tree.label: Internal: O'Reilly 2024 Analog
decision_tree.sub: Prior L2 override approved with peak-season SLA + Happy Returns bundle. Closed 14% above modeled OR. Bundle saves outperform price-match saves.

decision_tree.label: Companion Correlates
decision_tree.sub: HIGH — $41M at risk · Override precedent risk · Renewal due Jul 30. S2 delivers higher profit ($14.4M vs $11.8M) AND avoids portfolio-wide override precedent.

decision_tree.label: Options: Happy Returns Bundle / L2 Override / Decline
decision_tree.sub: A: S2 Happy Returns bundle (OR 0.67, profit $14.4M, within guardrails). B: S3 floor override (OR 0.70, profit $11.8M, precedent risk). C: Decline — accept churn ($41M lost).

decision_tree.label: Matt Selected: Option A — S2 Happy Returns
decision_tree.sub: Directed analyst to re-stage with S2 as lead. Sent back without approving S3 override. Guardrail exists to prevent margin leakage disguised as retention.

decision_tree.label: In Reality
decision_tree.sub: S2 proposal delivered to AutoZone sourcing team Jun 14. Happy Returns attach confirmed viable with aftermarket volume. AutoZone evaluating.

decision_tree.label: Next Gate
decision_tree.sub: AutoZone decision expected before Jul 30 renewal date. L1 Accessorial cap tightening 20%→18% routed to VP Revenue Mgmt.

activity_log.change: AutoZone ($41M, Pkt 10941) flagged — FedEx counter-offer at −8.5% base discount with 24-month lock
activity_log.notes: Renewal due Jul 30 — narrow retention window with aftermarket peak season Aug–Oct

activity_log.change: AutoZone Q3 earnings: publicly stated 'reviewing carrier diversification' — confirms churn risk is real, not posturing
activity_log.notes: Public statement signals active evaluation — not just leverage negotiation

activity_log.change: Analyst T. Whitaker staged S0–S3. S3 matches FedEx at OR 0.70 — breaches L2 Tier ceiling TARGET 81.1%→143%
activity_log.notes: S3 was staged as lead recommendation despite guardrail breach — process concern

activity_log.change: S2 alternative: Happy Returns bundle, OR 0.67, within guardrails, profit $14.4M (+$2.6M more than S3)
activity_log.notes: Compliant option exists that is MORE profitable — S3 escalation was unnecessary

activity_log.change: O'Reilly Auto 2024 analog: L2 override with Happy Returns bundle closed 14% above modeled OR. Bundle saves consistently outperform price-match saves
activity_log.notes: Proven analog confirms bundle approach — reduces execution risk

activity_log.change: HIGH — $41M at risk, override precedent risk, Jul 30 deadline. Happy Returns is genuine fit with aftermarket return volume, not a concession
activity_log.notes: S2 is both more profitable AND avoids precedent — clear recommendation

activity_log.change: A: S2 Happy Returns (OR 0.67, $14.4M profit). B: S3 floor override (OR 0.70, $11.8M, precedent risk). C: Decline ($41M lost)
activity_log.notes: Option A recommended — higher profit, within guardrails, proven analog

activity_log.change: Matt directed analyst to re-stage with S2 as lead. S3 override NOT approved. Guardrail preserved.
activity_log.notes: Guardrail exists to prevent margin leakage disguised as customer retention — principle upheld

activity_log.change: S2 proposal delivered to AutoZone sourcing team. Happy Returns attach confirmed viable with aftermarket volume
activity_log.notes: AutoZone evaluating — decision expected before Jul 30 renewal date

activity_log.change: L1 Accessorial cap tightening 20%→18% routed to VP Revenue Mgmt for portfolio review
activity_log.notes: Systemic fix to prevent future override escalations like this one

title: Amazon Exit — Capacity Reallocation Pace Risk Review
body: ~500K ADV freed in Q1. SMB (34.5%) and Healthcare ($3B) absorbing well. Enterprise Automotive is the pace risk — 5 accounts $116M below plan. Q2 margin guide (7.5–8.5%) depends on both clearing.

decision_tree.label: Amazon Capacity Reallocation Review
decision_tree.sub: ~500K ADV freed in Q1. Absorption tracking across segments — Automotive is pace risk to Q2 margin guide.

decision_tree.label: External: Q1 2026 Earnings
decision_tree.sub: Amazon ADV reduced 500K pieces/day in Q1. Now 8.8% of revenue (from 13%+). June 2026 completion confirmed by CEO. 23 facilities closed.

decision_tree.label: External: FedEx + Market Signal
decision_tree.sub: FedEx +10% US domestic revenue in Q3 FY26. Targeting freed UPS enterprise capacity in Automotive with Auto Express product.

decision_tree.label: Internal: Segment Absorption Data
decision_tree.sub: SMB: 34.5% record. B2B: 45.2% (6-yr high). Healthcare: $3B first quarter. RPP +6.5% YoY. Premium mix shift working in 3 of 4 segments.

decision_tree.label: Internal: Enterprise Gap Tracker
decision_tree.sub: Automotive T1 accounts $116M below plan — freed enterprise capacity not absorbed as modeled. Root cause: Jan 2026 coverage reduction + FedEx timing.

decision_tree.label: Companion Correlates
decision_tree.sub: HIGH — Q2 margin guide 7.5–8.5% requires both: one-time costs clearing AND auto segment gap closing. Automotive is the single largest variable in H2 recovery math.

decision_tree.label: Options: Accelerate ABM / Accept Pace Risk
decision_tree.sub: A: Accelerate Automotive ABM + weekly tracking. Activate ABM ramp on Ford + Stellantis. B: Accept pace risk — over-rotate SMB + Healthcare to compensate.

decision_tree.label: Matt Selected: Option A — Accelerate ABM
decision_tree.sub: ABM ramp on Ford + Stellantis activated. Weekly margin dashboard commissioned. Ops reallocation scorecard requested.

decision_tree.label: In Reality
decision_tree.sub: ABM ramp active on Ford + Stellantis. NAAF pre-lock in motion. Q2 margin recovery depends on one-time cost clearance (confirmed non-recurring) + Automotive close rate.

decision_tree.label: Next Gate
decision_tree.sub: Q2 margin target 7.5–8.5%. H2 target 11–13% to reach 9.6% FY. Automotive account close rate by Aug 31 is the single biggest variable.

activity_log.change: Amazon ADV reduced 500K pieces/day in Q1 — now 8.8% of revenue (from 13%+). 23 facilities closed. June completion confirmed
activity_log.notes: Amazon exit on track — structural transformation proceeding as planned

activity_log.change: FedEx +10% US domestic revenue in Q3 FY26. FedEx Express targeting freed UPS enterprise capacity in Automotive with Auto Express
activity_log.notes: Competitive window narrowing — FedEx exploiting UPS transition

activity_log.change: SMB: 34.5% record. B2B: 45.2% (6-yr high). Healthcare: $3B first quarter. RPP +6.5% YoY. Premium mix shift working in 3 of 4 segments
activity_log.notes: Three segments absorbing ahead of schedule — structural thesis validated

activity_log.change: Automotive T1 accounts $116M below plan — freed enterprise capacity not absorbed as modeled. Root cause: Jan 2026 coverage reduction + FedEx timing
activity_log.notes: Only segment where reallocation is not converting to margin — pace risk identified

activity_log.change: Q2 margin guide 7.5–8.5% requires both one-time costs clearing AND auto segment gap closing. $116M gap is single largest variable in H2 recovery
activity_log.notes: 9.6% FY margin guide requires H2 margins of 11–13% — achievable only if Automotive closes gap

activity_log.change: A: Accelerate Automotive ABM + weekly tracking dashboard. B: Accept pace risk — over-rotate SMB + Healthcare to compensate
activity_log.notes: Option B compounds the Automotive problem — FedEx deepens penetration while UPS over-rotates to already-performing segments

activity_log.change: Matt approved Option A. ABM ramp on Ford + Stellantis activated. Weekly margin dashboard commissioned. Ops reallocation scorecard requested
activity_log.notes: Option A addresses root cause directly rather than compensating around it

activity_log.change: ABM ramp active. NAAF pre-lock in motion. One-time costs ($350M: MD-11 retirement, Ground Saver, weather) confirmed non-recurring by CFO
activity_log.notes: Q2 recovery depends on both cost clearance (confirmed) and Automotive close rate (in progress)

activity_log.change: $600M network savings realized in Q1. $3B structural savings program on track. Driver Choice reductions confirmed structural
activity_log.notes: Structural cost improvements are delivering — variable is commercial execution

activity_log.change: Q2 margin 7.5–8.5% range. H2 needs 11–13% for 9.6% FY guide. Automotive close rate by Aug 31 is single biggest variable
activity_log.notes: Weekly margin dashboard now live — tracking close rate vs +$40M P50 modeled lift

title: Board Pre-Read — Q2 2026 Commercial Strategy Narrative
body: Board meets June 18. Deck due June 11. Board will probe: Q1 margin drag, $89.7B recovery path, NAAF $50M ROI, FedEx posture. Narrative anchors on prior commitments delivered — SMB record, Healthcare $3B, Amazon exit on track.

decision_tree.label: Board Pre-Read Due June 11
decision_tree.sub: Board meets June 18. Deck must address Q1 margin drag, recovery path, NAAF ROI, and FedEx posture.

decision_tree.label: External: Board Agenda + Prior Transcript
decision_tree.sub: Q4 2025 commitments: Amazon exit, SMB record, Healthcare $20B path, 50 facilities closed H1. All delivered or on track. FedEx +10% growth on Board's radar.

decision_tree.label: External: FedEx + Macro Signal
decision_tree.sub: FedEx Freight spin-off Jun 1. FedEx Express +10% Q3 US domestic. Middle East fuel spike. 10% import surcharge extended to Sep 30.

decision_tree.label: Internal: Q1 2026 Performance
decision_tree.sub: Q1 margin 4.0% (adj.) — below Q2 guide 7.5–8.5%. $350M one-time costs: MD-11 retirement, Ground Saver, weather/casualty. CFO: 'largely behind us.'

decision_tree.label: Internal: Commercial Progress
decision_tree.sub: SMB 34.5% record. Healthcare $3B first quarter. B2B 45.2% (6-yr high). Amazon exit on track. Network savings $600M Q1. NAAF launches August.

decision_tree.label: Companion Correlates
decision_tree.sub: HIGH — Board credibility on $89.7B / 9.6% guide requires crisp narrative. All Q4 commitments delivered. Q1 drag explained by non-recurring costs. H2 mechanism must be specific.

decision_tree.label: Options: Lead with Commitments / Lead with Guide
decision_tree.sub: A: Lead with delivered commitments → explain Q1 → H2 mechanism → FedEx → NAAF. B: Lead with Q2 guide and H2 expectations first (risk: 'why believe the guide given Q1?').

decision_tree.label: Matt Selected: Option A — Commitments First
decision_tree.sub: Deck structure: prior commitments → Q1 drag → Q2 mechanism → FedEx → NAAF. CFO alignment completed before submission.

decision_tree.label: In Reality
decision_tree.sub: Deck submitted June 11. CFO narrative alignment completed. Four Board probe questions with prepared answers. NAAF ROI answer: Ford + Stellantis windows close before September.

decision_tree.label: Next Gate
decision_tree.sub: Board meeting June 18. Goal: Board exits with confidence in FY guide + H2 inflection narrative. Prior commitment tracker in slide 1 is key framing device.

activity_log.change: Board meeting June 18 — deck due June 11. Q1 margin 4.0% vs 7.5–8.5% guide will be probed
activity_log.notes: Credibility on $89.7B / 9.6% FY guide depends on narrative framing

activity_log.notes: All prior commitments delivered — this is the credibility anchor

activity_log.change: FedEx Freight spin-off Jun 1. FedEx Express +10% Q3 US domestic. Middle East fuel spike. Import surcharge extended to Sep 30
activity_log.notes: Board will probe competitive posture and macro impact on guide

activity_log.change: Q1 margin 4.0% (adj). $350M one-time costs: MD-11 retirement ($120M), Ground Saver ($95M), weather/casualty ($135M). CFO confirms non-recurring
activity_log.notes: Q1 drag is fully explained by non-recurring costs — narrative problem if framed wrong

activity_log.change: SMB 34.5%, Healthcare $3B, B2B 45.2%, Amazon 8.8%, RPP +6.5%. Network savings $600M. NAAF launches August
activity_log.notes: Structural thesis (yield over volume, SMB, Healthcare) is playing out in data

activity_log.change: Prior commitment delivery is credibility anchor. Q1 drag becomes problem only if framed wrong. H2 mechanism must be specific, not 'we expect improvement'
activity_log.notes: Board enters trusting strategy — narrative must reinforce, not undermine, that trust

activity_log.change: A: Lead with delivered commitments → Q1 explain → H2 mechanism. B: Lead with Q2 guide first (risk: back-footed on 'why believe?')
activity_log.notes: Option A lets Q1 explanation land as expected, not alarming

activity_log.change: Matt approved Option A. Deck structure set: commitments → Q1 drag → Q2 mechanism → FedEx → NAAF. CFO alignment required
activity_log.notes: Board confidence in H2 depends on believing Q1 was transitional — delivered commitment list is evidence

activity_log.change: Deck submitted June 11. CFO narrative alignment completed. Four Board probe questions with prepared answers drafted
activity_log.notes: NAAF ROI answer prepared: Ford + Stellantis windows close before September

activity_log.change: Board meeting June 18. Slide 1: prior commitments tracker. Prepared answers for: margin drag, recovery path, NAAF ROI, FedEx posture
activity_log.notes: Goal: Board exits with confidence in FY guide + H2 inflection narrative

title: Enterprise Pricing Discipline — L1 Accessorial Cap 22%→20%
body: Portfolio-wide ceiling tightened Aug 2025. Result: $42M actual vs $36M modeled (+17%). Margin realization +6pp. Win-rate compression −1.2pp (within tolerance). Policy now permanent.

decision_tree.label: Accessorial Margin Leakage Identified
decision_tree.sub: Portfolio audit flagged post-approval concessions as #1 margin leakage source. UPS at 22% ceiling was above market range.

decision_tree.label: External: Market Benchmark
decision_tree.sub: Carrier benchmark Q2 2025: 16–20% Off is market range for accessorial concessions. UPS at 22% ceiling drifted upward through successive negotiation rounds.

decision_tree.label: External: FedEx Pricing Posture
decision_tree.sub: FedEx applying DIM to 90% of shipments + tighter accessorial discipline. UPS 22% ceiling creating margin gap vs competitor posture.

decision_tree.label: Internal: Portfolio Audit — 187 Packets
decision_tree.sub: Post-approval accessorial concessions (FSC, RES, DAS) identified as #1 leakage. 84% realization vs 90%+ target. 172 packets affected.

decision_tree.label: Internal: Historical Close Rate Data
decision_tree.sub: Win-rate model: 2pp ceiling tightening = −1.5pp retention compression (tolerable) + $12M profit recovery. Prior L1 cycles confirmed model.

decision_tree.label: Companion Correlates
decision_tree.sub: HIGH — $12M per 2pp · 84%→90%+ realization target. Ceiling drift not visible until audit surfaced it. 172 packets already above new ceiling.

decision_tree.label: Options: Tighten to 20% / Tighten to 18%
decision_tree.sub: A: Tighten to 20% Off — immediate portfolio effect, modeled +$12M, −1.5pp win-rate. B: Tighten to 18% Off in one step — faster but −3pp win-rate risk.

decision_tree.label: Matt Selected: Option A — 20% Off Ceiling
decision_tree.sub: CFO co-signed. Analyst pool trained within 2 weeks. DA guardrails updated to flag any breach automatically. Sequencing over speed.

decision_tree.label: Resolved: $42M Actual vs $36M Modeled
decision_tree.sub: $42M actual (+17% above model). Margin realization +6pp. Win-rate compression −1.2pp (better than −1.5pp modeled). 172 packets rerated. Policy permanent.

decision_tree.label: Next Step: Tighten to 18%
decision_tree.sub: Next guardrail review cycle. Modeled additional recovery: +$12M. AutoZone (Pkt 10941) handled as single-use exception — not a ceiling change.

activity_log.change: Portfolio audit flagged post-approval accessorial concessions (FSC, RES, DAS) as #1 margin leakage source — 84% realization vs 90%+ target
activity_log.notes: Concessions added after formal packet approval — bypassing DA guardrail process

activity_log.change: Carrier benchmark: 16–20% Off is market range. UPS at 22% ceiling — above market, drifted upward through successive negotiation rounds
activity_log.notes: UPS ceiling above market range — unforced margin leakage

activity_log.change: FedEx applying DIM to 90% of shipments + tighter accessorial discipline. UPS 22% ceiling creating margin gap vs competitor
activity_log.notes: Competitor tightening while UPS drifting — gap widening

activity_log.change: 187 packets audited. 172 affected by ceiling change. Post-approval concessions identified in 84% of enterprise packets
activity_log.notes: Systemic issue — not isolated to specific analysts or accounts

activity_log.change: Win-rate model: 2pp tightening = −1.5pp retention compression + $12M profit recovery. Prior L1 cycles confirmed model accuracy
activity_log.notes: Model has track record — risk is quantified and within tolerance

activity_log.change: Tightening to 20% aligns to market, recovers $12M, within win-rate tolerance. Mechanism is clear and reversible if competitive data changes
activity_log.notes: Bigger risk was NOT acting — continued drift compounds realization gap

activity_log.change: A: Tighten to 20% (+$12M, −1.5pp win-rate). B: Tighten to 18% in one step (faster but −3pp risk, too aggressive mid-cycle)
activity_log.notes: Option B recommended as second step after Option A outcome measured

activity_log.change: Matt approved Option A (20% Off). CFO co-signed. Analyst pool trained within 2 weeks. DA guardrails updated to auto-flag breaches
activity_log.notes: Sequencing matters more than speed — 18% is right next step after this outcome confirmed

activity_log.change: $42M actual vs $36M modeled (+17%). Margin realization +6pp. Win-rate compression −1.2pp (better than −1.5pp modeled)
activity_log.notes: Outperformed model on both margin recovery and win-rate — policy now permanent

activity_log.change: 172 packets rerated, 6 of 8 above-ceiling packets renegotiated. Next step: tighten to 18% in next guardrail review cycle (+$12M additional)
activity_log.notes: AutoZone (Pkt 10941) handled as single-use exception — not a ceiling change

learnings.body: Hard policy ceiling with clear governance proved more effective than 'soft guidance' had been previously. The ceiling proved immediately learnable for the analyst pool — escalation churn was minimal within 4 weeks. The $42M actual vs $36M modeled (+17% outperformance) validates both the model and the implementation quality. Closed-won margin recovery was clean and visible within one quarter.

Win-rate compression on Retention saves (−1.2pp) was within tolerance but still created two packet withdrawals totaling approximately $8M opportunity cost. These were time-bound deals that couldn't wait for the grandfathering review. A 5-business-day expedite path for time-bound retention deals was not in place at go-live.

When implementing any pricing policy tightening, simultaneously publish: (1) the new ceiling, (2) a 5-business-day expedite review path for time-bound Retention deals, (3) a 6-month grandfathering window for deals already in late-stage Customer Counter. The expedite path prevents the withdrawal losses that ceiling changes create on time-sensitive packets.

title: SMB Digital Access Program — Q1 Acquisition Acceleration
body: SMB at record 34.5% of US volume. Three verticals targeted: Healthcare SMB ($380M headroom), Auto Aftermarket ($210M), High-Tech ($155M). FedEx post-spin threat pre-empted. Cohorts locked before Q3.

decision_tree.label: SMB Acquisition Window — FedEx Post-Spin
decision_tree.sub: 6–8 week window before FedEx can launch meaningful SMB digital campaign post Freight spin-off. $745M total vertical headroom.

decision_tree.label: External: FedEx Post-Spin Signal
decision_tree.sub: FedEx Freight spin-off Jun 1 frees Express management attention and capital. Ship Manager historically inferior to DAP but post-spin SMB digital investment is obvious next move.

decision_tree.label: External: Q1 2026 SMB Performance
decision_tree.sub: SMB 34.5% record (from 31.2% prior year). B2B 45.2% (6-yr high). SMB ADV grew despite overall US Dom ADV down 8% YoY. RPP +6.5%.

decision_tree.label: Internal: Vertical Headroom Model
decision_tree.sub: Healthcare SMB: $380M (Andlauer + Frigo-Trans cold-chain unique). Auto Aftermarket SMB: $210M (downstream of T1 ABM). High-Tech SMB: $155M (re-shoring).

decision_tree.label: Internal: DAP Cohort Performance
decision_tree.sub: Healthcare lifecycle campaigns: +12% above baseline. 1-to-many ABM (1,498 Tier-3 accounts) has proven response rate from T1 seed data.

decision_tree.label: Companion Correlates
decision_tree.sub: HIGH — 6-week pre-emption window · $745M total headroom. UPS has 3 advantages FedEx can't match quickly: Andlauer cold-chain, T1 auto network, DAP conversion data.

decision_tree.label: Options: 3-Vertical Simultaneous / Healthcare Only
decision_tree.sub: A: 3-vertical simultaneous DAP campaign (Healthcare + Auto Aftermarket + High-Tech). B: Healthcare SMB only — highest headroom, sequence others.

decision_tree.label: Matt Selected: Option A — 3-Vertical Launch
decision_tree.sub: Three-vertical simultaneous launch. Healthcare lifecycle + Auto Aftermarket 1-to-many ABM (1,498 accounts) + High-Tech re-shoring cohort. Two-week activation.

decision_tree.label: Resolved: Cohorts Performing Above Baseline
decision_tree.sub: Healthcare DAP +12%. Auto Aftermarket first-response +18%. High-Tech 90-day retention above baseline (weeks 45–70 churn flagged). Q2 SMB +60bps QoQ.

decision_tree.label: Next Step: Monitor FedEx Response
decision_tree.sub: Watch Q3 for FedEx digital SMB response. High-Tech weeks 45–70 retention: SLA to be added to DAP onboarding for capability-led cohorts.

activity_log.change: FedEx Freight spin-off Jun 1 creates 6–8 week window — FedEx Express will refocus on SMB digital. Ship Manager investment coming
activity_log.notes: Window is binding constraint — timing advantage over FedEx, not capability

activity_log.change: SMB at 34.5% record (from 31.2% prior year). B2B 45.2%. SMB ADV grew despite overall US Dom ADV down 8% YoY. RPP +6.5%
activity_log.notes: SMB momentum is real and accelerating — investment timing is right

activity_log.change: Vertical headroom: Healthcare SMB $380M (Andlauer cold-chain unique), Auto Aftermarket $210M (T1 ABM downstream), High-Tech $155M (re-shoring)
activity_log.notes: $745M total addressable — three verticals where UPS has structural advantages

activity_log.change: DAP conversion baselines by vertical available. Healthcare lifecycle: +12% above baseline. 1-to-many ABM (1,498 Tier-3) has proven response rate
activity_log.notes: Proven performance data reduces execution risk across all three verticals

activity_log.change: UPS has 3 advantages FedEx can't match quickly: Andlauer cold-chain (Healthcare), T1 auto relationship network (Aftermarket seeding), DAP conversion data
activity_log.notes: Activating cohort campaigns NOW locks loyalty before FedEx enters — waiting forfeits window

activity_log.change: A: 3-vertical simultaneous DAP campaign (all within 2 weeks). B: Healthcare only — highest headroom, sequence others later
activity_log.notes: Option B sequencing would allow FedEx to enter Auto Aftermarket and High-Tech uncontested

activity_log.change: Matt approved Option A. 3-vertical simultaneous launch. Healthcare lifecycle + Auto Aftermarket 1-to-many ABM (1,498 accounts) + High-Tech re-shoring
activity_log.notes: 6-week window is binding constraint, not execution capacity. Option B forfeits 2 of 3 verticals

activity_log.change: All three campaigns activated within 2-week target. Healthcare lifecycle, Auto Aftermarket ABM, High-Tech re-shoring cohort live
activity_log.notes: Execution on schedule — FedEx SMB digital campaign not yet visible in market

activity_log.change: Healthcare DAP +12% above baseline. Auto Aftermarket first-response +18%. High-Tech 90-day retention above baseline but weeks 45–70 churn higher than modeled
activity_log.notes: Two verticals exceeding targets. High-Tech churn flagged to product team for SLA fix

activity_log.change: Q2 SMB trending +60bps QoQ. FedEx SMB digital campaign not yet visible. High-Tech weeks 45–70: service-quality SLA to be added to DAP onboarding
activity_log.notes: Window strategy worked — monitor Q3 for FedEx response. High-Tech cohort fix in progress

learnings.body: The 1-to-many ABM downstream extension (using the T1 Automotive account list as a seed for Automotive Aftermarket SMB targeting) was a creative and capital-efficient move — it piggy-backed on existing industry relationships and event sponsorships to reach a 10× larger account set. Healthcare cold-chain thought leadership worked because Andlauer + Frigo-Trans capability is genuinely unique — DAP converts higher when the service differentiation is real.

90-day retention for the High-Tech SMB cohort, while above baseline, showed higher churn in weeks 45–70 than the model predicted. The re-shoring narrative drove signup but post-signup service experience in some tech-heavy zip codes had slower pickup windows than premium SMB customers expected. Product team alerted.

For any SMB cohort that is capability-led (cold-chain, hazmat, re-shoring), build a service-quality SLA for that specific cohort into the DAP onboarding flow from day 1 — not as a reactive retention measure. Cohorts with a specific capability value-prop churn when the capability promise isn't matched by the operational delivery in weeks 30–60.
